title: Publication 28 — Appendix B: Two-Letter State and Possession Abbreviations

chunks.heading: U.S. State Abbreviations
chunks.text: USPS two-letter state abbreviations: AL (Alabama), AK (Alaska), AZ (Arizona), AR (Arkansas), CA (California), CO (Colorado), CT (Connecticut), DE (Delaware), DC (District of Columbia), FL (Florida), GA (Georgia), HI (Hawaii), ID (Idaho), IL (Illinois), IN (Indiana), IA (Iowa), KS (Kansas), KY (Kentucky), LA (Louisiana), ME (Maine), MD (Maryland), MA (Massachusetts), MI (Michigan), MN (Minnesota), MS (Mississippi), MO (Missouri), MT (Montana), NE (Nebraska), NV (Nevada), NH (New Hampshire), NJ (New Jersey), NM (New Mexico), NY (New York), NC (North Carolina), ND (North Dakota), OH (Ohio), OK (Oklahoma), OR (Oregon), PA (Pennsylvania), RI (Rhode Island), SC (South Carolina), SD (South Dakota), TN (Tennessee), TX (Texas), UT (Utah), VT (Vermont), VA (Virginia), WA (Washington), WV (West Virginia), WI (Wisconsin), WY (Wyoming).

chunks.heading: U.S. Territories and Military
chunks.text: Territories and possessions: AS (American Samoa), GU (Guam), MP (Northern Mariana Islands), PR (Puerto Rico), VI (Virgin Islands), FM (Federated States of Micronesia), MH (Marshall Islands), PW (Palau). Military designations: AE (Armed Forces Europe, Middle East, Canada), AP (Armed Forces Pacific), AA (Armed Forces Americas excluding Canada).

chunks.heading: Geographic Directional Abbreviations
chunks.text: Directional abbreviations: N (North), E (East), S (South), W (West), NE (Northeast), SE (Southeast), NW (Northwest), SW (Southwest).
